ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Nachos Bhel or bhel puri fusion. FOODON_00002473 (snack food) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Snacks, savoury) is the closest defensible parent for this Indian street-food snack fusion. No exact code for bhel puri or nachos bhel exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to Nachos Bhel or bhel puri fusion snack. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Nachos Bhel weighing approximately 200 g, as typically served at an Indian street-food or fusion restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Bhel Puri (composite estimate) and Corn Tortilla Chips — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Tortilla chips, plain)
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nachos Bhel is a fusion dish combining corn nachos (tortilla chips) with bhel puri-style toppings (onion, tomato, peanuts, yogurt, tamarind chutney, mint chutney, spices). No single approved-source entry exists for this exact dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 40% corn nachos (USDA FoodData Central FNDDS/SR Legacy, NDB 19056, Tortilla chips plain, ~490 kcal/100g), 20% peanuts (ICMR-NIN), 15% whipped yogurt (ICMR-NIN), 15% onion and tomato (ICMR-NIN), 10% chutneys and spices (ICMR-NIN). Composite per-100g values derived from weighted average of these components. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; corn tortilla chips GI ~52; bhel puri analogue GI ~50–55
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nachos Bhel. GI estimated at 52 based on: (1) corn tortilla chips GI ~52 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables), which forms the dominant carbohydrate base (~40% by weight); (2) bhel puri analogues (puffed rice-based chaat) with GI ~50–55 per Sydney GI Database. Weighted composite GI estimated at 52, classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per 200g serving = (52 × 35.8 × 2.0) / 100 = 37.2g. GL is high per serving due to large carbohydrate portion.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100g; ~620 mg per 200g serving) from corn nachos, tamarind chutney, and chaat masala
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100g or less, as a full 200g serving provides approximately 620 mg sodium (27% of the 2300 mg daily limit), and should request reduced-salt chutney preparations where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~52) but high glycemic load per serving (~37g GL per 200g plate) due to large carbohydrate portion from corn nachos
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index is low-moderate at 52, the high glycemic load per standard serving (approximately 37g GL) means individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-portion (approximately 100g) and pair with additional protein or fibre to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content (~12.5g/100g) with saturated fat (~2.1g/100g) from nachos and yogurt; sodium load from chutneys and spices
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the combined sodium (~620 mg per serving) and fat content; the presence of peanuts provides beneficial monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats, but portion control is recommended to keep saturated fat and sodium within daily targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nachos Bhel is a contemporary Indian-fusion street food snack that combines the crunch of flaky corn nachos (tortilla chips) with the bold, tangy flavours of traditional Indian bhel puri. The dish is assembled — not cooked — by layering crispy corn nachos with finely diced raw onion, fresh tomato, roasted peanuts, and a generous dollop of whipped yogurt (dahi), then drizzled with sweet-sour tamarind chutney and vibrant green mint chutney, and finished with chaat masala and cumin powder. The result is a multi-textural experience: crunchy nachos contrast with creamy yogurt and juicy tomato, while the interplay of tamarind's tartness, mint's freshness, and chaat masala's umami-spice creates the characteristic chaat flavour profile. Nachos Bhel is popular in Indian fusion restaurants and street-food outlets across Singapore, where it bridges South Asian chaat culture with the globally familiar nacho format. Nutritionally, a 200g serving provides approximately 560 kcal, 7.2g protein per 100g, 12.5g fat per 100g, and 35.8g carbohydrate per 100g. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), though the glycemic load per full serving is high (~37g), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is moderate-to-high due to nachos, chutneys, and spice blends. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable but contains peanuts (allergen) and dairy (yogurt). It is not suitable for vegans or those with peanut or dairy allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Nachos Bhel?
What are the ingredients in Nachos Bhel?
Is Nachos Bhel good for diabetics?
What is the glycemic index of Nachos Bhel?
Is Nachos Bhel the same as bhel puri?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Nachos Bhel as a named dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using ICMR-NIN (Indian foods: bhel puri components, peanuts, yogurt, vegetables) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (corn tortilla chips). GI is estimated from analogous foods in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved primary sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nachos Bhel is a fusion dish not found as a named entry in any approved primary nutrient database. All per-100g values are composite estimates derived from weighted component analysis (see nutrient_source). 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN or USDA SR Legacy component sources for this dish's ingredients at the level of detail available. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for the Indian food components used in this estimate. 4. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for corn tortilla chips and peanuts; these are approximate. 5. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input record appears to be a field survey platform tagging error — Nachos Bhel contains no chicken and is a vegetarian dish; this tag has been disregarded. 6. GI of 52 is classified as low per the ≤55 threshold rule. Glycemic load per 200g serving is approximately 37.2g, which is high, and this is clinically relevant for diabetes management. 7. Allergen note: peanuts are a major allergen; dairy (yogurt) is present; corn nachos may contain wheat depending on manufacturer — gluten-free status is not confirmed.
